title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement abigailmabe03@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to brpubVJCE
text: outlines propose change brpubVJCE. detailed discussion contributing tidyverse packages, please see development contributing guide code review principles.

title: Contributing to brpubVJCE
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to brpubVJCE
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to brpubVJCE
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("BR-Visualization/brpubVJCE", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to brpubVJCE
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to brpubVJCE
text: Please note brpubVJCE project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Control Fonts — control_fonts
text: returns list font sizes : p - normal text elements within ggplot theme. h1 - titles. h2 - subtitles. label - text elements outside ggplot theme. want add annotation label plot, use font size label. default, renders 10pt. rel - legend elements.

what: Create Forest and Dot Plots for Treatment Effects — create_forest_dot_plot
title: Create Forest and Dot Plots for Treatment Effects — create_forest_dot_plot
text: Generates side--side forest dot plots specified outcomes, grouped factor type. Displays treatment effects, confidence intervals, optional clinical thresholds.

title: Create Forest and Dot Plots for Treatment Effects — create_forest_dot_plot
code: create_forest_dot_plot(   data,   outcomes_with_thresholds = NULL,   treatment1 = "Drug A",   treatment2 = "Placebo",   filter_value = "None",   precalculated_stats = FALSE,   forest_upper_limit = NULL )

title: Create Forest and Dot Plots for Treatment Effects — create_forest_dot_plot
text: data data frame prepared using prepare_forest_dot_data() matching structure. outcomes_with_thresholds Either NULL (uses available outcomes thresholds), character vector outcome names include (thresholds), named list names outcomes values thresholds. lists, directions default "greater" positive values "less" negative values, can specified list(outcome = list(threshold = 0.1, direction = "greater")). treatment1 Character; label first treatment group (default: "Drug "). treatment2 Character; label second treatment group (default: "Placebo"). filter_value Character; value used filter Filter column (default: "None"). precalculated_stats Logical; TRUE, skips calculation uses provided statistics. forest_upper_limit Numeric; optional upper limit forest plot, adds reference line value provided.

title: Create Forest and Dot Plots for Treatment Effects — create_forest_dot_plot
text: patchwork object containing combined dot forest plots shared legend.

title: Create Forest and Dot Plots for Treatment Effects — create_forest_dot_plot
code: # First, prepare the data prepared_data <- prepare_forest_dot_data(effects_table)  # Generate the plot using all available outcomes with no thresholds dotforest <- create_forest_dot_plot(prepared_data) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ggsave_custom("dotforest.png", imgpath = "./", inplot = dotforest, dpi = 300) } # }  # Use only specific outcomes with no thresholds create_forest_dot_plot(prepared_data,   outcomes_with_thresholds = c("Benefit 1", "Benefit 2") ) #> `height` was translated to `width`. #> `height` was translated to `width`.   # Custom thresholds with automatic direction detection create_forest_dot_plot(prepared_data,   outcomes_with_thresholds = list(     "Benefit 1" = 0.10,     "Benefit 2" = 0.08,     "Risk 1" = -0.05   ) ) #> `height` was translated to `width`. #> `height` was translated to `width`. #> `height` was translated to `width`.   # Custom thresholds with explicit directions create_forest_dot_plot(prepared_data,   outcomes_with_thresholds = list(     "Benefit 1" = list(threshold = 0.10, direction = "greater"),     "Risk 1" = list(threshold = -0.05, direction = "less")   ) ) #> `height` was translated to `width`. #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Trade-off plot — generate_tradeoff_plot
code: generate_tradeoff_plot(   data,   filter,   category,   benefit,   risk,   type_risk,   type_graph,   ci,   ci_method,   cl,   mab,   mar,   threshold,   ratio,   b1,   b2,   b3,   b4,   b5,   b6,   b7,   b8,   b9,   b10,   r1,   r2,   r3,   r4,   r5,   r6,   r7,   r8,   r9,   r10,   testdrug,   type_scale,   lower_x,   upper_x,   lower_y,   upper_y,   chartcolors )

title: Trade-off plot — generate_tradeoff_plot
text: data (data.frame) input dataset following variables required columns. Note variables Grouped_Outcome, Statistics, Outcome_Status required generating trade-plot, listed required columns key generating value tree, starting point subsequent benefit-risk assessments. Factor: character vector containing whether outcome "Benefit" "Risk" Grouped_Outcome: character vector containing name grouped outcomes, e.g., Infections Outcome: character vector containing name outcomes, e.g., Herpes viral infections, upper respiratory tract infections Statistics: character vector containing summary statistics outcomes, e.g., %, mean change baseline Type: character vector containing whether outcome "Binary" "Continuous" variable Outcome_Status: character vector containing whether outcome "Identified" "Potential" outcome Filter: character vector containing filter subgroup data, "None" filtre applicable. Example: None; Sex. Category: character vector containing  category filtering subgroup data, "" filter applicable. Example: ; Male, Female. Trt1: character vector containing name active treatments Trt2: character vector containing controlled term "Placebo" Drug_Status: character vector containing whether treatment "Approved" "Test" drug following variables situational columns - Filled specific summary statistic related row outcome (ex. proportion): Rate_Type: numeric vector containing whether AE rate "EventRate" "IncRate". Required risk outcomes displayed exposure-adjusted event rate incidence rate. Prop1: numeric vector containing proportion active treatment. Required binary outcomes displayed proportions; can populated nSub1/N1 nSub1 N1 provided. IncRate1: numeric vector containing exposure-adjusted incidence rate per 100 PYs active treatment. Required risk outcomes displayed exposure-adjusted incidence rates; can populated nSub1/PYAR1*100 nSub1 PYAR1 provided. EventRate1: numeric vector containing exposure-adjusted event rate per 100 PYs active treatment. Required risk outcomes displayed exposure-adjusted event rates; can populated nEvent1/PEY1*100 nEvent1 PEY1 provided. Mean1: numeric vector containing mean active treatment. Required continuous outcomes. Prop2: numeric vector containing proportion comparator treatment. Required binary outcomes displayed proportions; can populated nSub2/N2 nSub1 N1 provided. IncRate2: numeric vector containing exposure-adjusted incidence rate per 100 PYs comparator treatment. Required risk outcomes displayed exposure-adjusted incidence rates; can populated nSub2/PYAR2*100 nSub1 PYAR1 provided. EventRate2: numeric vector containing exposure-adjusted event rate per 100 PYs comparator treatment. Required risk outcomes displayed exposure-adjusted event rates; can populated nEvent2/PEY2*100 nEvent1 PEY1 provided. Mean2: numeric vector containing mean comparator treatment. Required continuous outcomes. following variables optional columns - Can either hand entered calculated package (ex. confidence intervals): N1: integer vector containing total number subjects active treatment. Required needing calculate confidence intervals within package proportions. 100PYAR1: numeric vector containing 100 patient-years risk active treatment. Required needing calculate confidence intervals within app exposure-adjusted incidence rates. 100PEY1: vector containing 100 patient-years exposure active treatment. Required needing calculate confidence intervals within app exposure-adjusted event rates. Sd1: numeric vector containing standard deviation active treatment. Required needing calculate confidence intervals within app continuous outcomes; can populated Se1/SQRT(N1) Se1 N1 provided. N2: integer vector containing total number subjects comparator treatment. Required needing calculate confidence intervals within package proportions. 100PYAR2: numeric vector containing 100 patient-years risk comparator treatment. Required needing calculate confidence intervals within app exposure-adjusted incidence rates. 100PEY2: numeric vector containing 100 patient-years exposure comparator treatment. Required needing calculate confidence intervals within app exposure-adjusted event rates. Sd2: numeric vector containing standard deviation comparator treatment. Required needing calculate confidence intervals within app continuous outcomes; can populated Se2/SQRT(N2) Se2 N2 provided. Diff_LowerCI: numeric vector containing lower confidence interval difference proportions continuous outcomes. Required using supplied confidence intervals difference proportions continuous outcomes. Diff_UpperCI: numeric vector containing upper confidence interval difference proportions continuous outcomes. Required using supplied confidence intervals difference proportions continuous outcomes. Diff_IncRate_LowerCI: numeric vector containing lower confidence interval difference exposure-adjusted incidence rates. Required using supplied confidence intervals difference exposure-adjusted incidence rates. Diff_IncRate_UpperCI: numeric vector containing upper confidence interval difference exposure-adjusted incidence rates. Required using supplied confidence intervals difference exposure-adjusted incidence rates. Diff_EventRate_LowerCI: numeric vector containing lower confidence interval difference exposure-adjusted event rates. Required using supplied confidence intervals difference exposure-adjusted event rates. Diff_EventRate_UpperCI: numeric vector containing upper confidence interval difference exposure-adjusted event rates. Required using supplied confidence intervals difference exposure-adjusted event rates. RelRisk_LowerCI: numeric vector containing lower confidence interval relative risk binary outcomes. Required using supplied confidence intervals relative risk binary outcomes. RelRisk_UpperCI: numeric vector containing upper confidence interval relative risk binary outcomes. Required using supplied confidence intervals relative risk binary outcomes. OddsRatio_LowerCI: numeric vector containing lower confidence interval odds ratio binary outcomes. Required using supplied confidence intervals odds ratio binary outcomes. OddsRatio_UpperCI: numeric vector containing upper confidence interval odds ratio binary outcomes. Required using supplied confidence intervals odds ratio binary outcomes. following variables supplementary columns - Used calculate columns required package(ex. number subjects events): nSub1: integer vector containing number subjects events active treatment. required; can used calculate Prop1 nSub1/N1. Dur1: numeric vector containing duration treatment active treatment. required; can used estimate 100PYAR1 100PEY1. nEvent1: integer vector containing number events active treatment. required; can used calculate EventRate1 nEvent1/100PEY1. Se1: numeric vector containing standard error active treatment. required; can used calculate Sd1 Se1*SQRT(N1). nSub2: integer vector containing number subjects events comparator treatment. required; can used calculate Prop2 nSub2/N2. Dur2: numeric vector containing duration treatment comparator treatment. required; can used estimate 100PYAR2 100PEY2. nEvent2: integer vector containing number events comparator treatment. required; can used calculate EventRate2 nEvent2/100PEY2. Se2: numeric vector containing standard error comparator treatment. required; can used calculate Sd2 Se2*SQRT(N2). following variables documentation columns - Record data source (ex. Study xyz, Table 1.2.3, date): MCDA_Weight: numeric vector containing MCDA weight Population: character vector containing population analysis (e.g., ITT, Safety Set) Data_Source: character vector containing source data (e.g., Reference CSR Table xxx) Quality: character vector containing quality data Notes: character vector containing notes filter (character) selected filter category (character) selected category benefit (character) selected benefit outcome risk (character) selected risk outcome type_risk (character) selected way display risk outcomes (crude proportions, Exposure-adjusted rates (per 100 PYs)) type_graph (character) selected way display binary outcomes (Absolute risk, Relative risk, Odds ratio) ci (character) selected choice display confidence intervals (Yes, ) ci_method (character) selected method display confidence intervals (Supplied, Calculated) cl (numeric) confidence level mab (numeric) specified minimum acceptable benefit mar (numeric) specified maximum acceptable risk threshold (character) selected way set benefit-risk threshold (None, Straight line, Segmented line, Smooth curve) ratio (numeric) specified maximum acceptable ratio risk benefit b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7, b8, b9, b10 (numeric) specified benefit r1, r2, r3, r4, r5, r6, r7, r8, r9, r10 (numeric) specified risk tolerance testdrug (character) selected choice display test drug (Yes, ) type_scale (character) selected scale display type (Fixed, Free) lower_x, upper_x, lower_y, upper_y (numeric) specified axis limits chartcolors (vector) vector colors, number levels number treatments

title: Simulate data (utilized for function tests) — gensurv
text: seed numerical input object set standard data simulation. n1 numerical input object represents desired total population simulated data (treatment group 1). n2 numerical input object represents desired total population simulated data (treatment group 2). obsv_duration numerical input object maximum time observational period span simulated data. lambda1 numerical input object first lambda used data simulation (used first parameter simulation). lambda2 numerical input object second lambda used data simulation (used second parameter simulation). unit non-numerical input specifies length observational period ("Week", "Month", etc.).

title: Combine the cumulative excess plot and corresponding table into one figure — gensurv_combined
code: gensurv_combined(   df_plot,   df_table,   subjects_pt,   visits_pt,   fig_colors_pt = c("#0571b0", "#ca0020"),   titlename_p =     "Cumulative Excess # of Subjects w/ Events\n                             (per 1000 Subjects)",   mar,   mab,   mcd,   rel_adjust = 0.12,   rel_heights_table = c(1, 0.4),   ben_name_p = "Primary Efficacy",   risk_name_p = "Recurring AE",   legend_position_p = c(-0.03, 1.15) )

title: Combine the cumulative excess plot and corresponding table into one figure — gensurv_combined
text: df_plot dataframe 6 variables named following: eventtime: vector time points event occurred. diff: vector containing difference active control effects. obsv_duration: variable specifies duration observational period (numerical). obsv_unit: variable specifies unit duration observational period (non-numerical input). outcome: vector containing whether outcome "Benefit" "Risk". eff_diff_lbl: vector containing label effect difference. df_table dataframe 6 variables named following: obsv_duration: variable specifies duration observational period (numerical). n: vector containing number subjects experienced event given time (numerical). effect: specifies active control effect. outcome: specifies whether outcome classified "Benefit" "Risk" (must either "Benefit" "Risk" values). eff_code: 0 control 1 active effect. subjects: vector containing total number active/placebo subjects study given time. subjects_pt numerical input specifies baseline proportion subjects study. visits_pt numerical input length observational periods. fig_colors_pt Allows user change colors figure (defaults provided). Must vector length 2, color corresponding benefit first risk second. titlename_p Allows user change documentation title (default provided) mar maximum acceptable risk treatment, discussed team, must numerical. mab minimum acceptable benefit treatment, discussed team, must numerical. mcd minimum clinically important difference treatment, discussed team, must numerical. rel_adjust Allows user specify figure table alignment. Must single number, corresponding space left figure, relative figure's width (denoted 1). rel_heights_table Elements fig vs table size. ben_name_p Allows user specify benefit interest (default provided). risk_name_p Allows user specify risk interest (default provided). legend_position_p Allows user specify legend position. Must vector length 2, first value corresponding position legend relative x-axis, second corresponding position legend relative y-axis (numeric).

code: library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2)  xxx <- tribble(   ~x, ~z, ~w, ~y,   1, "BOLD_AA", " plain", 1,   2, "b", "b", 0,   3, "c", "c", 0 ) ggplot(xxx, aes_string(x = "x", y = "z")) +   geom_point() +   scale_y_discrete(     label = labs_bold(cond = xxx[["y"]], xxx[["z"]], nonbold = xxx[["w"]])   ) #> [2025-09-23 08:59:41] > Dataout object from #> the labs_bold function is created

title: brpubVJCE 0.0.0.1
text: Supports continuous binary outcomes Computes direction-aware differences confidence intervals Handles precalculated raw input formats Shows comparative treatment effects CI bars Visualizes thresholds clinical relevance Custom color-coding direction markers (e.g., ← Favours Placebo, Favours Drug →)

title: brpubVJCE 0.0.0.1
text: Uses ggplot2, patchwork, ggtext flexible plotting Input validation helpful error messages Ready use publications Shiny apps
